prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5). A close view of a potter's hands shaping a tall wet-clay pot, the spinning lump catching warm light, in a north-east Indian (Naga) village work-shed. Bamboo cylinders, a basket of grey-red clay, and finished pots drying in the background. Warm amber afternoon light through an open shed door, dark earthen interior. Loose luminous watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain, glowing against the dark ground. No text, no labels.
alt: A potter's hands shaping a wet clay pot on a wheel, warm light, north-east Indian village setting

prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5). A young Naga girl of about ten stands at the edge of a village work-shed, watching two expert potters shape clay by hand. Her eyes are full of longing and concentration. Pots drying on a bed of hay nearby, hills visible through the shed opening. Warm late-afternoon light, dark earthen interior tones. Loose luminous watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain, glowing against the dark ground. No text, no labels.
alt: A young girl watching expert potters at work in a village shed, her eyes full of longing

Loose luminous watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain, glowing against the dark ground. No text, no labels.
